beats.activity: Wakes up and does a quick morning routine at home before getting the day’s dog-sitting gear ready.; Drinks an espresso and checks messages and routes for the morning pet visits.
beats.reasoning: Full energy to start; he likes being prepared for early walks and sits.

beats.reasoning: He prefers calm, reliable routines between appointments.

diary:
This morning started easy—woke up at home in Sammamish, made my espresso, and checked messages like always, but also kept glancing at the route map like it mattered. By the time I swung into Pine Lake Park for that first walk, the sun felt already warm on my face, and the dogs were in that “let’s go, world!” mood. Downtown Bellevue was buzzing when I headed that way for drop-ins, and I squeezed in back-to-back check-ins at Bellevue Downtown Park before lunch. At Paseo Cafe I grabbed something quick, then somehow the whole day slowed at The Old Fashioned when the USA vs Wales match kicked off—laughing with friends between commercials felt like a little reward I didn’t plan for.
Afterwards I worked Marymoor Park, stocked up at PetSmart in Redmond, and then came home to pottery and painting. The light stayed kind until evening, and I ended the day feeling steady and good.
thought_process: I planned my schedule around small practical things: early starts for the dogs, buffers for traffic, and keeping the midday open so I could eat without rushing. The light rail closure between Westlake and University District meant I leaned more on driving time and didn’t try to “optimize” too hard—better to arrive calm than late. The World Cup match absolutely tugged at me; I timed lunch to be near enough to downtown that I could watch at The Old Fashioned, and it lifted my mood during the stretch when I’d otherwise feel purely task-focused. Once I got back to Sammamish, I deliberately chose pottery and painting over screens, because my brain needed a softer landing after all that leash-and-water logistics. My mood shifted from energized to social to peaceful.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
